publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: Adopted daughter of Reinhard Oppel and his first wife Käthe Oppel (née Brebeck); first child of Reinhard Oppel.

Presumably when Reinhard and Käthe divorced in 1910 Ingeborg and her younger brother, Wolf Dietrich Oppel (1908–92), were assigned to the custody of their mother. Ingeborg features sporadically in the correspondence between Reinhard and Heinrich Schenker: in May 1931 Reinhard reports that she has passed her first state examination and become a candidate for higher Civil Service (OJ 13/10, [129]), in which capacity she moves to Hamburg. In July she is reported as having a chronic recurring liver condition. By September 1932 she has submitted her doctoral thesis and is working in a criminal court in Moabit (Berlin), aiming to become an assessor; by March 1935 she is working for the Ministry of Justice to develop a new juvenile law. Nothing is known of her after that.
